main_turf.bias_notes: Anti-clockwise oval layout located in the Limestone Coast region of South Australia. Features slight undulations heading into a short 320m home straight. The short run-in creates a distinct advantage for tactical, on-pace runners ('E' and 'P' styles) who can settle in the first four before turning for home. Deep closers often struggle unless mid-race pace is extreme.
main_turf.post_bias: Inside barriers (1-4) offer a noticeable advantage in sprint trips up to 1200m due to the tight run to the first turn. Wide draws (8+) over 1000m and 1200m are penalizing unless the runner possesses enough early speed to cross cleanly without burning excess energy.

handicapping_angles.horses_for_courses_specialists: Heavily upgrade local specialists with established Limestone Coast regional form (Naracoorte, Penola, Mount Gambier, Bordertown). Pay close attention to runners dropping down from Morphettville/Adelaide midweek provincial class to regional country grade.
handicapping_angles.trainer_jockey_trends: Look for leading South Australian border and Western Victorian country stables targeting Naracoorte cup and fixture days.
